rules.title: A CI pipeline definition exists
rules.lesson: every other delivery rule presupposes an automated pipeline; "we run checks by hand" is the failure mode the standard exists to retire. Pure presence — deterministic
rules.rationale: Every other delivery rule presupposes an automated pipeline; "we run checks by hand" is the failure mode the entire standard exists to retire. Pure presence/absence over known CI paths — deterministic.
rules.fix: Add a CI definition (e.g. .github/workflows/ci.yml) that builds and tests on push/PR.

rules.title: The pipeline builds & tests on PR/push
rules.lesson: scoped to what a line-scan proves: your checked-in pipeline declares build+test on integration triggers. Merge-gating is BR-03; test quality is baseline TEST; external CI escapes via risk-acceptance (R1)
rules.rationale: The claim is scoped precisely to what a line-scan proves: your checked-in pipeline definition declares build+test on integration triggers. A build-only workflow runs zero tests, so a bare build is deliberately absent from the pattern. External/unreadable CI false-negatives in one direction only and resolves via risk-acceptance, never silently.
rules.fix: Add trigger keys and a test step to the workflow; external/unreadable CI → pipeline jdg new --kind risk-acceptance --subject CI-02 --reason "CI is hosted at <system>, definition not in-repo" --review-by <date>.

rules.title: Deployment is automated
rules.lesson: deploy-by-hand is the named failure source; the citation holds with no team-size precondition, so this blocks even at solo. Manual-by-design is a documented deviation. Library SKIPs (publishing ≠ deployment)
rules.rationale: Deploy-by-hand is the named failure source, and the citation holds with no team-size precondition — so this blocks even at solo. Scoped claim: a deploy step/workflow is declared in the checked-in pipeline definitions. A genuinely manual-by-design deploy is a documented deviation.
rules.fix: Add a deploy job/workflow (promote the same artifact the pipeline built); manual-by-design → pipeline jdg new --kind deviation --subject CI-03 --reason "..." --review-by <date>.

checks.pattern: environment:|\bdeploy\b|aws .*deploy|flyctl deploy|vercel|netlify deploy|kubectl apply|terraform apply|serverless deploy|pulumi up|cdk deploy

rules.title: A post-deploy smoke test runs in the deploy path
rules.lesson: DORA defines the deployment pipeline as including a deployment test — the deploy path is incomplete without it. Presence-of-mechanism only; adequacy folds into ENV-06 sign-off
rules.rationale: DORA defines the deployment pipeline as including a deployment test — the deploy path is incomplete without it. Presence-of-mechanism check only; smoke-test adequacy folds into ENV-06’s sign-off residue. Heuristic: no YAML parser, step ordering inferred by line position.
rules.fix: Add a smoke step to the deploy workflow that hits the service’s health endpoint and fails the job on non-2xx.

rules.title: The pipeline is alive at the forge
rules.lesson: a forge-auto-disabled workflow files nothing forever while every dashboard stays green — liveness is a checked fact. Lifts baseline OPS-07 workflow-state at the delivery workflows
rules.rationale: A scheduled/triggered workflow the forge has auto-disabled files nothing forever while every dashboard stays green — the death mode is silence, so liveness is a checked fact, not an assumption. One recorded forge query per CI workflow file; state active passes, any other state fails naming the state.
rules.fix: gh workflow enable <file> (or the Actions UI); a commit or manual dispatch resets the inactivity clock.

rules.title: Deploy jobs depend on green build/test jobs
rules.lesson: a deploy that can run against a red build ships the failure; in-workflow job ordering is the declared form of "each stage gates the next". Line-scan (no YAML parser) → stays warning at every tier (R2)
rules.rationale: A deploy that can run against a red build ships the failure; in-workflow job ordering is the declared form of "each stage gates the next". Line-scan structural inference (no YAML parser), hence heuristic — and hence a warning at every tier (R2: the blocker-at-critical hardening was not locked).
rules.fix: Add needs: [build, test] (the repo’s actual job names) to the deploy job.
